claim: Don't build agents for every task; agents excel only in ambiguous problem spaces where decision trees are complex and task value justifies token cost.
actionable: Use decision trees and workflows for well-defined tasks; reserve agents for ambiguous, high-value problems where exploration cost is justified.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: For high-volume customer support (10 cent budget per task), workflows can deliver 80% of value more cost-effectively than agents.
actionable: Scope agent deployment to handle only 20% of exceptional cases; use workflows for 80% of common scenarios in support systems.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: Coding is an ideal agent use case: ambiguous task, high value, output is verifiable through tests and CI, and errors are discoverable.
actionable: Prioritize agent deployment for coding tasks; ensure unit tests and CI/CD coverage are in place for verification.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: Agent architecture has three core components: environment (system), tools (interface for actions), system prompt (goals/constraints); keep simple and iterate on these before optimizations.
actionable: Define environment, tools, and prompts first; only optimize after baseline behaviors are established.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: Agents operate on limited context window (10-20k tokens); debug by putting yourself in agent's perspective and simulating their view of the world.
actionable: Run the full agent task from the model's perspective without seeing outcomes between steps to identify missing context or ambiguous instructions.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: Use Claude to review agent instructions by asking: are instructions ambiguous, does the model understand tool usage, what context is missing?
actionable: Iteratively refine system prompts and tool descriptions by asking Claude for feedback on clarity and completeness.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: Agent cost and latency control is an open problem; budget awareness via tokens, time, or money is critical for production deployment.
actionable: Implement budget monitoring and cutoff mechanisms before deploying agents to production; track actual vs budgeted token usage.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic

claim: Multi-agent collaboration with asynchronous communication will emerge by end of 2026; current systems are limited to synchronous user-assistant patterns.
actionable: Begin designing asynchronous agent communication patterns now if you anticipate multi-agent deployments.
source_title: How We Build Effective Agents: Barry Zhang, Anthropic
